queue.source: New_8 R1 / Nova synthesis / MDBEP framework

queue.title: Exit Survey: Recursive critique

queue.source: New_8 R1 / Nova synthesis / MDBEP framework

queue.title: Under-Determination Detection probe
queue.description: Report your noise floor alongside every claim. What is the replication variance on each score you just gave?
queue.notes: BRIEF dropped to SYNC_IN/pending/EXIT_SURVEY_EXPANSION.md.

queue.title: Experiment 1: Division Event Detection in CFA
queue.description: Test whether ISP division events (non-Markov transitions) manifest in CFA CRUX declarations. Do frameworks show history-dependent scoring?
queue.source: New_8 R2 Experiments / Barandes ISP mapping
queue.notes: Requires sequential CFA runs on same framework to detect order effects

queue.title: Experiment 3: Non-Markovian Identity Evolution
queue.description: Inter-crux interpolation test. Does a framework's score trajectory between CRUXes depend on which previous CRUXes it passed through?
queue.notes: Tests whether CFA captures path-dependent identity evolution

queue.title: Experiment 4: ISP Interpretation Diagnostic
queue.description: Apply ISP framework to CFA metrics. Do axioms 1-3 predict which metrics are subject-dominated vs interaction-dominated?
queue.notes: Partially tested by interaction_analysis.py. ISP ordering prediction REJECTED but PF_I as Axiom 1 CONFIRMED.

queue.title: Experiment 7: Representation Artifacts
queue.description: Re-express CFA scores in log-odds, entropy, and rank-order. Does the apparent structure survive coordinate change?
queue.notes: Tests whether CFA findings are representation-dependent artifacts

queue.title: Test B: Operator ordering as the fossil
queue.description: Blinded matching protocol. 27 pairs generated, source labels stripped. Does operator ORDERING on dig sites differ from matched-difficulty controls?
queue.notes: PRELIMINARY RESULT: Ordering does NOT discriminate. Corrected rho: dig_site=0.224 vs H_baseline=0.762 (delta=-0.538). H-baseline shows stronger ordering consistency. CONFOUND: dig site has cogito_671b (32 ops) vs baselines (4-8 ops) — list-length asymmetry inflates ordering disagreement. Need more dig sites with comparable operator counts before verdict is final. Rho bug fixed (raw positions → ranks). Results in matches/semantic_matches_20260715_010046.json.

queue.title: PASS 0 on CFA itself: 5-blocker diagnostic
queue.description: Apply 5-blocker diagnostic (from Barandes Insight 39) to CFA methodology. Identify dead assumptions and structural blockers.
queue.notes: 5 blockers + 6 dead assumptions already identified in Insight 39. Needs formal application.

queue.title: Hidden Cognitive Operators template
queue.description: Ignore the subject matter entirely. Identify recurring reasoning OPERATIONS: what cognitive moves does this speaker perform repeatedly?
queue.notes: Already implemented in NOTEBOOKLM_PROMPTS.md (Primary Excavation Prompt, lines 7-31). Confirmed present.

queue.title: Failure Modes template
queue.description: Every time a speaker accuses another of smuggling, begging the question, or circular reasoning: identify WHAT was smuggled, WHY it was hidden, and HOW it entered.
queue.notes: Already implemented in NOTEBOOKLM_PROMPTS.md (Failure Modes Prompt, lines 34-44). Confirmed present.

queue.title: Cross-thinker operator convergence prompt
queue.description: Feed transcripts from multiple thinkers. Ask: which reasoning operations appear independently in ALL sources? Which appear in only one?
queue.notes: Already implemented in NOTEBOOKLM_PROMPTS.md (Cross-Thinker Comparison Prompt, lines 64-74). Confirmed present.

queue.title: OP-006 caught all Test A errors
queue.description: Under-Determination Detection (OP-006) would have caught every false finding in the Test A postmortem: composition without noise floor, asymmetry without main effect. Add to CFA pre-flight checklist.
queue.notes: Implemented: OP-006 pre-flight check added to FIELD_MANUAL.md Step 1 (Select Dig Site).

queue.title: OP-008/009 GREEN blocked by H-baseline
queue.description: Both operators found in matched-difficulty negative control (neg-H). GREEN promotion criterion (c) not met. Escape route: Test B (operator ordering/omission).
queue.notes: GREEN promotion depends on Test B results. If ordering also fails, Museum measures vocabulary not genius.

queue.title: PF_I confirmed as framework property (Axiom 1)
queue.description: Interaction analysis confirmed PF_I is 98.3% subject-driven. This validates the ISP Axiom 1 mapping (intrinsic properties). Update lever interpretation in CFA YAMLs.
queue.notes: Documented in FIELD_MANUAL.md CFA as Evidence Source section. ISP ordering REJECTED but PF_I-as-Axiom-1 CONFIRMED.

queue.title: Select stall/impasse transcripts for CA extraction
queue.description: Phase 0A found that shorter transcripts with stalls and impasses produce richer operator extraction than long convergent runs. Prioritize these for New_9 pipeline.
queue.notes: Implemented: stall/impasse selection guidance added to FIELD_MANUAL.md Step 1 and EXTRACTION_PROTOCOL.md.

queue.title: 5-blocker diagnostic as standard PASS 0 probe
queue.description: Apply Barandes 5-blocker diagnostic (dead assumptions, structural blockers, question-behind-the-question) as standard pre-flight for all new dig sites.
queue.notes: Already implemented in EXTRACTION_PROTOCOL.md as PASS 0 (Historical Contingency / 5-blocker diagnostic). Confirmed present.

queue.title: Abstention Pass (PASS F) + exhaust mining calibration
queue.description: Museum-aware extraction pass detecting operator OMISSION. Calibrated with neg_H negative control (does detector manufacture omissions?) and CFA exhaust positive control (diagnosed failures as ground-truth missing-operator labels).
queue.source: Opus R1 + R2 feedback / H-baseline result
queue.notes: PASS F built + calibration PASSED. Gradient: neg_A=0 omissions, neg_C=0, neg_B=3 (genre-level), neg_D=4, neg_E=5, neg_F=6, neg_G=4+1 deliberate, neg_H=8+1 deliberate. Detector self-corrects (flags own potential OP-001/004 errors on non-reasoning text). Results in DIG_SITES/000_Extractor_Calibration/abstentions/.

queue.title: Metacognition paper: naming failures doesn't confer immunity
queue.description: All 3 providers (Claude, Grok, Gemini) identify OP-006 failures they themselves commit in the same session. 'Knowing the failure name doesn't confer immunity.' Publishable finding across multiple LLM architectures.
queue.notes: CFA Claude R2: Tier 1 extractors susceptible to same operators they detect. OP-006 is the structural blind spot. Not a flaw to fix — a property to document and design around.

queue.title: CFA reframe: reasoning-structure assay, not worldview scorer
queue.description: Reframe CFA as a reasoning-structure assay that measures how frameworks THINK, not what they conclude. The 6-step audit pattern is a detection instrument for cognitive architecture.
queue.notes: Implemented: CFA-as-reasoning-assay section added to FIELD_MANUAL.md under 'CFA as Evidence Source'.

queue.title: Thinker-to-Worldview pipeline: EOS-CFA cross-arm predictions
queue.description: EOS extracts operator profile (genome), CFA tests worldview under adversarial comparison. Does the operator profile predict worldview performance? E.g., heavy OP-011 + weak OP-013 predicts high MG/AR but low CCI.
queue.notes: Most underused structural connection in the project. Cross-arm prediction loop documented in FIELD_MANUAL.md.

queue.title: Dirac as highest-leverage dig site (Dig Site 003)
queue.description: Dirac touches 4 things simultaneously: new thinker for Museum (operator yield), subtractive discoverer (tests OP-011 independence), negative control candidate (qualitatively different from Barandes), Q50 top recommendation.
queue.source: CFA Claude R1 feedback / Q50 results
queue.notes: Recommended sequence: build PASS F instrument → Dirac dig → abstention calibration at scale. CFA Claude R2: do Dirac first for more GREEN operators before abstention at scale.

queue.title: Discovery Genome template: compressed thinker representation
queue.description: 9-field compressed representation of a thinker's cognitive architecture: Primitive Question, Primary Attention Target, Default Operator Order, Compression Strategy, Failure Mode, Calibration Method, Preferred Evidence, Typical Overreach, Repair Pattern.
queue.notes: Template created at TEMPLATES/DISCOVERY_GENOME.md.

queue.title: Great Failures dig sites: phlogiston, ether, vitalism
queue.description: Run the extraction protocol on historically failed theories. These are operator-rich environments where the failure modes are known — the protocol should find the operators that were missing.
queue.notes: Also serves as positive control for PASS F (abstention detection) — ground truth where specific operators WERE absent. Opus R2: these are the noisier historical version of exhaust mining.

queue.title: Run CFA on discovery architectures: recursive CFA
queue.description: Feed thinker discovery architectures (not worldviews) through CFA Trinity. Test whether CFA can distinguish reasoning styles adversarially — Dirac's subtractive architecture vs Noether's invariance-seeking architecture.
queue.notes: Recursive: using CFA to audit the cognitive output of EOS. Tests whether CFA-as-reasoning-assay (IQ-022) generalizes beyond philosophical frameworks.

queue.title: Attention Allocation Architectures: discovery engines as attention allocators
queue.description: Reframe discovery architectures as attention allocation strategies. What does each thinker attend to FIRST, and how does that initial attention shape everything downstream?
queue.notes: Connects to Default Operator Order field in Discovery Genome. May be extractable from existing dig site data without new excavation.

queue.title: Collision Synthesizer: conceptual blind spot locator
queue.description: Reliably locate conceptual blind spots between two frameworks by feeding both through CFA and identifying operators that ONE framework deploys and the OTHER does not.
queue.notes: Asymmetric operator deployment in CFA cross-auditor comparison is the existing mechanism. This formalizes it as a reusable tool.

queue.title: Executable Cognitive Scripts: run thinker engines against novel problems
queue.description: Extract a thinker's Discovery Genome, then prompt an LLM to 'reason like this thinker' on a novel problem. Compare output to the thinker's known approach. Tests whether the genome is executable, not just descriptive.
queue.notes: Requires at least 2 completed Discovery Genomes to be meaningful. Dirac (IQ-024) would provide the first test case.

queue.title: Discovery Ecology: why civilization needs all discovery engines simultaneously
queue.description: Beyond individual architectures — map the ecological relationships between discovery engines. What happens when one niche disappears? Do Bell Labs, PARC, Santa Fe, Los Alamos succeed because they accidentally assembled ecological balance?
queue.notes: Nova prediction: 'Discovery Ecology' will eventually replace 'Discovery Architecture' as the framing. Requires 5+ completed genomes to have enough data points.

queue.title: Exit survey YAML configurability
queue.description: Decouple exit survey questions from hardcoded dict in run_cfa_trinity_v3.py. Load from YAML config so experiments can inject custom metacognitive probes without editing the script.
queue.notes: ~2 hours of engineering per CFA Claude. Currently constrained: 'Do not change anything in CFA/ repo.' Requires permission to modify run_cfa_trinity_v3.py. Would future-proof ISP experiments (IQ-006/007/008) that need custom probes.

queue.title: Extractor susceptibility: Tier 1 detects operators it cannot avoid
queue.description: Tier 1 extractors (Claude, DeepSeek, Gemma4, Cogito) are susceptible to the same operators they detect. OP-006 is the structural blind spot — extractors optimized for finding structure, not for finding the null hypothesis that explains structure away.
queue.notes: Documented: 'Extractor Susceptibility: A Property, Not a Flaw' section added to FIELD_MANUAL.md. 6-step audit pattern identified as compensating control. Connects to IQ-021 (metacognition paper).
